arts.ttl: Man City have got £63m transfer priority right after late Brentford collapse
arts.sum: Manchester City threw away a two-goal lead at Brentford to surrender a couple of points and kill off any possiblity of getting back into the title race.

arts.ttl: Why Arsenal's new £51m man Martin Zubimendi is a coup for Mikel Arteta - and not just because the 'next Rodri' chose the Gunners over Liverpool, Man City and Real Madrid
arts.sum: LEWIS STEELE: Zubimendi is a down-to-earth character born and bred in the same region as Mikel Arteta, a true son of San Sebastian who reneged on a decision to join Liverpool this summer.

arts.ttl: Emotional Pep Guardiola rages at goalkeeper Stefan Ortega in bizarre embrace after Man City blew a two-goal lead one day after manager's 'split with wife' was revealed
arts.sum: Post-match, Guardiola was snapped in a bizarre embrace with his goalkeeper, Ortega, as he appeared angered by what had transpired, though continued to hug the German.

arts.ttl: Brentford 2-2 Man City: Pep Guardiola berates stars as City throw away two-goal lead
arts.sum: City’s short-lived revival came to an end on Tuesday night

arts.ttl: Phil Foden makes worrying admission after Man City throw away two-goal lead at Brentford
arts.sum: Phil Foden blamed Manchester City's fitness as they struggled to contain Brentford in the final half hour as the Bees came from 2-0 down to secure a point on Tuesday

arts.ttl: Leyton Orient beat Derby on penalties to set up Man City tie
arts.sum: Watch highlights as Leyton Orient beat Derby County 6-5 on penalties and progress to face Manchester City in Round four of the FA Cup.

arts.ttl: Frank hails Brentford mentality after Man City draw
arts.sum: Boss Thomas Frank praises Brentford's mentality and bravery with the ball after a dramatic 2-2 draw with Manchester City in the Premier League.

arts.ttl: FA Cup: Leyton Orient set up massive Man City tie after beating Derby County on penalties
arts.sum: The League One side overcame Championship opposition to set up a lucrative fourth round tie

arts.ttl: Man City crumble again as Slot's bold changes pay off
arts.sum: Just when you think Manchester City have this winning football games thing figured out, they take two steps further away from another Premier League title. Brentford - who went into the game with second-best home record in the league after Liverpool - is by no means an easy place to go.

arts.ttl: Frank praises ‘brave’ Brentford after fightback stuns City
arts.sum: Thomas Frank hailed his Brentford side after they pulled off a stunning late comeback to draw 2-2 with Manchester City. A Phil Foden brace looked to have won the game for the reining Premier League champions before goals from Yoane Wissa and Christian Norgaard levelled the contest.
